Shengming Zhou

A student of UofM building a GitHub-backed personal homepage for projects, notes, and whatever comes next.

Now Student

Building out a simple personal space on GitHub to introduce myself and collect projects over time.

Location
Ann Arbor / Remote
Focus
Study, projects, and personal writing

About

Keep the intro direct.

A homepage works better when it explains your current direction, not your full history.

I am Shengming Zhou, a student of UofM. This site is a small home for my background, projects, and notes as I keep learning and building.

The structure is intentionally simple: a clear introduction, selected work, and a place to publish ideas or progress over time.

Selected Work

Show a few things with enough context.

Three strong projects with clear summaries are usually better than ten undifferentiated cards.

2026

Portfolio System

featured

A content-driven personal site architecture focused on speed, writing, and maintainable project showcases.

Astro TypeScript Tailwind CSS
Open project

2026

GitHub Tooling Workflow

featured

A deployment flow using GitHub Actions and Pages so the site updates on every push to the default branch.

GitHub Actions GitHub Pages CI/CD

Notes

Reserve space for writing from day one.

Even if you only publish occasionally, the site should already have a place for thinking in public.

2026-04-13

What This Homepage Is For

A short note about treating a personal site as a living record of projects, writing, and current interests.

Link the profiles you actually use.
